stories.title: Megan Grant at the ESPYs — Garrett Wins, Softball Gains
stories.angle: The loss doesn't diminish what happened. Grant's 42 HRs earned a spot in the same conversation as generational performances from the NFL, Winter Olympics, and marathon. For softball, that visibility is the win.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Myles Garrett wins Best Record-Breaking Performance at the ESPYs.

Megan Grant didn't win. But her 42 HRs at UCLA — breaking a 31-year NCAA record — put softball on the same ballot as the NFL and Winter Olympics.

That's the win for the sport.

#ESPYs #Softball #MeganGrant

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Myles Garrett wins Best Record-Breaking Performance.

Megan Grant didn't win. But her 42 HRs at UCLA — breaking a 31-year NCAA record — were on the same ballot as an NFL sack record and 6 Olympic gold medals.

For softball, that's the win.

#ESPYs #Softball #MeganGrant

research.facts:
ESPYs Aftermath — Garrett Wins, Grant's Nomination Stands
**Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- 34th ESPYs held July 15, 2026, David H. Koch Theater, Lincoln Center, NYC — CONFIRMED (ESPN, TSN, KBTX)
- Host: Marcello Hernández — CONFIRMED (multiple press sources)
- Best Record-Breaking Performance winner: Myles Garrett (LA Rams), 23 NFL sacks in 2025 season — CONFIRMED (ESPN, TSN, KBTX; award presented by Chloe Kim and Tiffany Haddish)
- Megan Grant did NOT win — CONFIRMED (grant was nominated; Garrett's win confirmed by multiple sources)
- Grant's nomination: 42 HR at UCLA, NCAA Division I single-season record — CONFIRMED (UCLA Athletics)
- Other nominees: Johannes Høsflot Klæbo (6 Winter Games golds), Sabastian Sawe (marathon) — CONFIRMED
- Garrett also won Best NFL Player — CONFIRMED (2 ESPYs on the night)
- Best Male Athlete: Jalen Brunson (New York Knicks) — CONFIRMED (ESPN full winners list)
- Best Female Athlete: A'ja Wilson (Las Vegas Aces) — CONFIRMED
- Best Play: Shohei Ohtani (6 scoreless IP, 10 K, 3 HR in one game) — CONFIRMED
- Muhammad Ali Sports Humanitarian: Stephen Curry — CONFIRMED
- Arthur Ashe Award: Jason Collins (posthumous) — CONFIRMED
- Jimmy V Award: Jim Abbott — CONFIRMED
**Sources:**
- TSN: Myles Garrett wins Best Record-Breaking Performance at the ESPYs (2026-07-16)
- ESPN: Full list of every ESPYS 2026 award winner
- KBTX: Myles Garrett wins two ESPY awards
- UCLA Athletics: Vote Now: Betts, Grant Nominated for the 2026 ESPYs

stories.title: Utah Talons Clinch 2026 AUSL Postseason Berth
stories.angle: The Talons are the class of the league. Faraimo, Allchin, and Palacios built this lead — and now it's clinched.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
The Utah Talons are IN.

7th inning. Down a run. Jadelyn Allchin homers to tie. Sharlize Palacios follows with a 2-run blast.

Megan Faraimo: complete game, 10 strikeouts.

Utah Talons 5, OKC Spark 4.

First team to clinch the 2026 AUSL postseason. 🥎

#AUSL #Softball #UtahTalons

research.facts:
Utah Talons Clinch AUSL Postseason Berth
**Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Utah Talons first team to clinch 2026 AUSL postseason berth — CONFIRMED (AUSL official site, OurSports Central, MLB.com)
- Clinch game result: Utah Talons 5, OKC Spark 4 — CONFIRMED
- 7th inning: Jadelyn Allchin hit her 4th HR to tie; Sharlize Palacios hit 2-run blast to take lead — CONFIRMED (AUSL official, OurSports Central)
- Megan Faraimo: complete game, 10 strikeouts — CONFIRMED
- Utah Talons record: 14-6 — CONFIRMED (standings as of that clinch; subsequent games may have changed)
- No. 1 seed earns direct bye to Championship Series at Davis Diamond (July 25) — CONFIRMED
- July 15 result: Carolina Blaze 12, Chicago Bandits 6 — CONFIRMED (MEDIUM — score confirmed, details from recap headline)
- OKC Spark 7, Texas Volts 2 (July 15) — CONFIRMED (Just Women's Sports; Jailyn Ford complete game, 8 K)
- Chicago Bandits current record: ~13-8 after July 15 loss — CALCULATED from 13-7 pre-game
- Portland Cascade: 11-9 entering July 16 (game vs Talons July 15 result not indexed) — MEDIUM
- Regular season ends July 20 — CONFIRMED
- Play-in (2nd vs. 3rd seed): July 23 — CONFIRMED
- Championship Series: July 25, Davis Diamond, Texas A&M, College Station TX — CONFIRMED
- Game 1 on ABC (first pro softball game on network TV) — CONFIRMED
**Sources:**
- AUSL Official: Utah Talons Clinch 2026 AUSL Postseason Berth
- OurSports Central: Utah Talons Clinch 2026 AUSL Postseason Berth
- MLB.com: Utah Talons headed to 2026 AUSL playoffs
- Just Women's Sports: Jailyn Ford Spins Complete AUSL Game as OKC Spark Top Texas Volts

stories.title: AUSL Thursday Night — Bandits vs. Blaze + Talons vs. Cascade
stories.angle: The regular season is running out. The Bandits need a response tonight after getting beat by the Blaze. The Talons play for nothing but pride and momentum heading into the postseason.

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
AUSL Thursday night:

🥎 Bandits vs. Blaze — 7 PM ET (Durham, NC)
🥎 Talons vs. Cascade — 10 PM ET (Hillsboro, OR)

Chicago lost to Carolina 12-6 last night. Tonight is the road rematch.

4 games left in the regular season. Every one counts.

#AUSL #Softball #ChicagoBandits

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Utah Talons vs. Portland Cascade is starting now.

10 PM ET. Hillsboro Ballpark. Hillsboro, OR.

Talons already clinched. Cascade needs this one to hold the third playoff spot.

#AUSL #Softball #PortlandCascade

stories.title: Why the Texas Volts Called Stanford to Help NiJaree Canady
stories.angle: This isn't a failure story — it's a development story. The Volts are investing in Canady because they believe in the talent. But the adjustment from college to professional pitching is real, and she's navigating it in public.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
NiJaree Canady in college: 104-24, 1.09 ERA, 1,127 K.

In the AUSL: 0-3, 6.87 ERA.

The Texas Volts reached out to ex-Stanford coach Tori Nyberg. Injuries at Texas Tech shifted her mechanics.

A development story, not a failure story.

#AUSL #Softball #NiJareeCanady

research.facts:
NiJaree Canady Pro Struggles — Volts Call Stanford
**Facts (MEDIUM-HIGH to HIGH confidence):**
- Canady selected No. 2 overall in 2026 AUSL College Draft (May 4, 2026) — CONFIRMED
- Signed with Texas Volts: June 12, 2026 — CONFIRMED (KCBD)
- Current AUSL record: 0-3 — CONFIRMED (theausl.com player page)
- ERA: 6.87 — CONFIRMED (theausl.com player page, SI.com)
- Earned runs allowed: 17; HR allowed: 7 — CONFIRMED
- Texas Volts reached out to former Stanford pitching coach Tori Nyberg — CONFIRMED (Sports Illustrated)
- Reason: Injuries at Texas Tech altered mechanics/body positioning — CONFIRMED (SI.com)
- College career: 104-24, 13 saves, 1.09 ERA, 1,127 K in 796.0 IP — CONFIRMED (Texas Tech Athletics)
- Final college season (Texas Tech, 2026): 29-7, 1.87 ERA — CONFIRMED
- 2025 season: 34-7, 1.11 ERA — CONFIRMED
- Named to Time Magazine Top 100 Most Influential Athletes in 2026 — CONFIRMED
- Texas Volts record after July 15: ~6-16 — CALCULATED/CONFIRMED (team standings, low confidence)
**Sources:**
- Sports Illustrated: Why the Texas Volts Called Stanford to Help NiJaree Canady
- AUSL player page: theausl.com/players/nijaree-canady/
- KCBD: NiJaree Canady officially signs with Texas Volts

stories.title: PGF Nationals — 3 Days Out
stories.angle: Three days. Rosters locked. The biggest travel ball showcase of the summer starts Sunday.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
PGF Nationals opens in 3 days.

Sunday, July 19. Orange County Great Park, Irvine, CA.

18U and 16U brackets go first. Rosters locked. September 1 recruiting window is 46 days away.

Biggest travel ball showcase of the summer.

#PGFNationals #Softball #TravelBall

research.facts:
PGF Nationals — 3 Days Out
**Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- PGF Nationals Week 1: July 19-25, Orange County Great Park, Irvine CA — CONFIRMED (pgfnationals.com)
- Divisions Week 1: 18U Platinum, 16U Premier, 16U Platinum — CONFIRMED
- Additional venues: Bill Barber Park (Irvine), Harvard Park (Irvine) — CONFIRMED
- Roster deadline: July 14, 2026 at 11:59 PM — CONFIRMED
- Week 2: July 26–August 2, 14U/12U/10U divisions, opening ceremonies at Huntington Beach Central Park — CONFIRMED
- Coverage: PGF.TV — CONFIRMED
- Arizona Storm Appel: finished 3rd in 2025 16U championship, 2026 roster features Alabama, Auburn, Oklahoma, Northwestern commits — CONFIRMED (On3 preview)
- Torynn Slaughter (Alabama), Peyton Valentine (Auburn), Payton Westra (Oklahoma), Goose Goodwin (Northwestern) — CONFIRMED
- September 1 = Class of 2028 recruiting contact window (46 days away) — CALCULATED from July 16
